title: ai-bookkeeper amount convention & ADR 0001
content: ai-bookkeeper ledger: Transaction.amount is a signed number — positive=income/credit, negative=expense/debit (relied on by wealth-score/compute.ts and format.ts). USD, 2 decimals. ADR 0001 (Accepted) added a pure-function validation boundary (features/ledger/lib/amount.ts + ingest.ts) with $1M per-transaction ceiling, rejecting non-numeric/negative-magnitude/zero/>2dp/out-of-range. Integer-cents representation was DEFERRED (not rejected) to a future ADR when Cloud SQL persistence lands — that's the durable fix for float drift; extend rather than contradict. Repo convention: co-located pure validation libs under features/*/lib exported via barrel. No unit-test runner wired (only Playwright e2e).

title: Session capture (Stop) — 2026-05-27 07:20 UTC
ai-bookkeeper ledger amount-validation review (ADR 0001)
content: Reviewed amount.ts/ingest.ts validation boundary. APPROVED with notes. Sign convention confirmed in code: wealth-score/lib/compute.ts keys income/expense on amount>0 / <0, so rejecting zero amounts at parse is correctness-critical (prevents a $0 row landing on the wrong branch). Open finding to recheck next review: parseAmount uses Number(cleaned) after stripping [$,\s], which accepts scientific/hex/binary literals ("1e5"->100000) — recommended a /^\d+(\.\d{1,2})?$/ guard; should be in place before the live SMS/OCR adapter (ADR task 5) feeds untrusted strings. Also: ingest validates only amount, not date/confidence/customerId (adjacent scope). Tests exist (12 cases) but tsx/CI runner not wired (ADR task 4) so they don't run yet.
